question: [File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Gate or ratifier bypass allowing restricted markets, restricted liquidation, or restricted credit/debt increase to be accessed by unauthorized users'] [Function: setIsRootRatified] Can an attacker ratify a root for an offer with offer.callback = address(attackerContract) to redirect maker callback execution, under PRECONDITIONS where maker authorized attacker and attacker constructs offer with callback = attackerContract, triggering CALL_SEQUENCE: attacker calls setIsRootRatified(maker, root, true) for offer with callback = attackerContract -> taker calls midnight.take(offer, ...) -> attackerContract.onBuy/onSell is called with maker's funds -> attackerContract performs unauthorized actions, violating INVARIANT that callbacks must not corrupt partial state through reentrancy, causing scoped impact: unauthorized fund redirection via attacker-controlled callback in ratified offer? Proof idea: unit test with malicious callback contract and assert maker funds cannot be redirected.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Gate or ratifier bypass allowing restricted markets, restricted liquidation, or restricted credit/debt increase to be accessed by unauthorized users'] [Function: setIsRootRatified] Can an attacker ratify a root for an offer with offer.receiverIfMakerIsSeller = address(attacker) to redirect seller proceeds, under PRECONDITIONS where maker authorized attacker and attacker constructs sell offer (buy=false) with receiverIfMakerIsSeller = attacker, triggering CALL_SEQUENCE: attacker calls setIsRootRatified(maker, root, true) for sell offer with receiverIfMakerIsSeller = attacker -> taker calls midnight.take(offer, ...) -> sellerAssets are sent to attacker instead of maker, violating INVARIANT that user funds must not be stolen or seized outside valid protocol rules, causing scoped impact: unauthorized theft of maker's loan token proceeds? Proof idea: unit test asserting that receiverIfMakerIsSeller in attacker-ratified offer can redirect maker's funds.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Gate or ratifier bypass allowing restricted markets, restricted liquidation, or restricted credit/debt increase to be accessed by unauthorized users'] [Function: setIsRootRatified] Can an attacker ratify a root for an offer with offer.ratifier = address(attackerRatifier) to substitute a malicious ratifier, under PRECONDITIONS where maker authorized attacker and attacker constructs offer with ratifier = attackerRatifier, triggering CALL_SEQUENCE: attacker calls setIsRootRatified(maker, root, true) for offer with ratifier = attackerRatifier -> take() checks isAuthorized[offer.maker][offer.ratifier] -> if maker has not authorized attackerRatifier, take reverts with RatifierUnauthorized, violating INVARIANT that ratifier must be authorized by maker, causing scoped impact: ratifier substitution bypass? Proof idea: unit test asserting that offer.ratifier must be authorized by maker regardless of SetterRatifier state.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Gate or ratifier bypass allowing restricted markets, restricted liquidation, or restricted credit/debt increase to be accessed by unauthorized users'] [Function: setIsRootRatified] Can an attacker ratify a root for an offer in a market with enterGate where the gate checks the buyer address, and the attacker is the buyer (taker of sell offer), under PRECONDITIONS where market.enterGate.canIncreaseCredit(buyer) returns false for attacker, triggering CALL_SEQUENCE: attacker calls setIsRootRatified(maker, root, true) for buy offer -> attacker calls midnight.take(buyOffer, ratifierData, units, attacker, ...) as taker -> gate check: canIncreaseCredit(buyer=maker) is checked for buy offer -> attacker as taker/seller has debt increased, not credit, violating INVARIANT that enter gate must restrict all credit/debt increases, causing scoped impact: unauthorized debt increase for non-whitelisted taker via buy offer? Proof idea: state test with WhitelistGate asserting gate checks correct party (buyer vs seller) for each offer direction.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Gate or ratifier bypass allowing restricted markets, restricted liquidation, or restricted credit/debt increase to be accessed by unauthorized users'] [Function: setIsRootRatified] Can an attacker ratify a root for an offer where buyerCreditIncrease == 0 (buyer already has debt >= units) to bypass the enterGate.canIncreaseCredit check, under PRECONDITIONS where buyer has existing debt >= units so buyerCreditIncrease = zeroFloorSub(units, buyerPos.debt) = 0, triggering CALL_SEQUENCE: attacker ensures buyer has debt >= units -> attacker calls setIsRootRatified(maker, root, true) -> attacker calls midnight.take(buyOffer, ...) -> gate check: buyerCreditIncrease == 0 so canIncreaseCredit is not called -> take succeeds without gate check, violating INVARIANT that gate checks must apply to all entry paths, causing scoped impact: gate bypass for credit increase when buyer has pre-existing debt? Proof idea: state test asserting gate is not called when buyerCreditIncrease == 0 and verify this is intended behavior.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Gate or ratifier bypass allowing restricted markets, restricted liquidation, or restricted credit/debt increase to be accessed by unauthorized users'] [Function: setIsRootRatified] Can an attacker ratify a root for an offer where sellerDebtIncrease == 0 (seller has enough credit to cover units) to bypass the enterGate.canIncreaseDebt check, under PRECONDITIONS where seller has existing credit >= units so sellerDebtIncrease = units - sellerCreditDecrease = 0, triggering CALL_SEQUENCE: attacker ensures seller has credit >= units -> attacker calls setIsRootRatified(maker, root, true) -> attacker calls midnight.take(sellOffer, ...) -> gate check: sellerDebtIncrease == 0 so canIncreaseDebt is not called -> take succeeds without gate check, violating INVARIANT that gate checks must apply to all entry paths, causing scoped impact: gate bypass for debt increase when seller has pre-existing credit? Proof idea: state test asserting gate is not called when sellerDebtIncrease == 0 and verify this is intended behavior.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Gate or ratifier bypass allowing restricted markets, restricted liquidation, or restricted credit/debt increase to be accessed by unauthorized users'] [Function: setIsRootRatified] Can an attacker ratify a root for a zero-unit take (units = 0) to bypass gate checks entirely, under PRECONDITIONS where units = 0 so buyerCreditIncrease = 0 and sellerDebtIncrease = 0, triggering CALL_SEQUENCE: attacker calls setIsRootRatified(maker, root, true) -> attacker calls midnight.take(offer, ratifierData, 0, taker, ...) -> gate checks are skipped because both increases are 0 -> maker callback executes with zero units, violating INVARIANT that gate checks must apply to all entry paths, causing scoped impact: callback execution in gated market without gate check via zero-unit take? Proof idea: unit test asserting zero-unit take triggers callback without gate check and verify callback cannot cause unauthorized state changes.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Gate or ratifier bypass allowing restricted markets, restricted liquidation, or restricted credit/debt increase to be accessed by unauthorized users'] [Function: setIsRootRatified] Can an attacker ratify a root for an offer in a market with both enterGate and liquidatorGate, then use the ratified offer to enable a liquidation callback that bypasses the liquidatorGate, under PRECONDITIONS where market has liquidatorGate and attacker constructs offer with callback that calls midnight.liquidate(), triggering CALL_SEQUENCE: attacker calls setIsRootRatified(maker, root, true) -> attacker calls midnight.take(offer, ...) -> maker callback calls midnight.liquidate(market, ...) -> liquidatorGate.canLiquidate(callback) is checked, violating INVARIANT that liquidator gate must not be bypassed through alternate liquidation or callback paths, causing scoped impact: unauthorized liquidation via callback in ratified offer? Proof idea: unit test with liquidatorGate and callback that calls liquidate, assert gate check applies to callback address.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Gate or ratifier bypass allowing restricted markets, restricted liquidation, or restricted credit/debt increase to be accessed by unauthorized users'] [Function: setIsRootRatified] Can an attacker ratify a root for an offer with offer.market.enterGate = address(0) (no gate) when the actual market was created with a non-zero enterGate, under PRECONDITIONS where market ID is determined by all market params including enterGate, triggering CALL_SEQUENCE: attacker calls setIsRootRatified(maker, root, true) for offer with market.enterGate = address(0) -> attacker calls midnight.take(offer, ...) -> touchMarket creates a new market with no gate -> take succeeds in ungated market, violating INVARIANT that gate checks must apply to all entry paths, causing scoped impact: unauthorized credit/debt increase in ungated shadow market? Proof idea: unit test asserting that offer.market.enterGate = address(0) creates a separate ungated market ID.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Gate or ratifier bypass allowing restricted markets, restricted liquidation, or restricted credit/debt increase to be accessed by unauthorized users'] [Function: setIsRootRatified] Can an attacker ratify a root for an offer with offer.market.liquidatorGate = address(0) (no liquidator gate) when the actual market was created with a non-zero liquidatorGate, under PRECONDITIONS where market ID includes liquidatorGate field, triggering CALL_SEQUENCE: attacker calls setIsRootRatified(maker, root, true) for offer with market.liquidatorGate = address(0) -> attacker calls midnight.take(offer, ...) -> touchMarket creates a new market with no liquidator gate -> attacker can liquidate in this shadow market, violating INVARIANT that liquidator gate must not be bypassed, causing scoped impact: unauthorized liquidation in shadow market with no liquidator gate? Proof idea: unit test asserting that offer.market.liquidatorGate = address(0) creates a separate market ID with no liquidation restriction.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Gate or ratifier bypass allowing restricted markets, restricted liquidation, or restricted credit/debt increase to be accessed by unauthorized users'] [Function: setIsRootRatified] Can an attacker ratify a root for an offer with modified LLTV or oracle params to create a more permissive market, under PRECONDITIONS where attacker constructs offer with market.collateralParams having higher LLTV than the intended market, triggering CALL_SEQUENCE: attacker calls setIsRootRatified(maker, root, true) for offer with modified collateralParams -> attacker calls midnight.take(offer, ...) -> touchMarket creates a new market with attacker's params -> attacker borrows more than intended LLTV allows, violating INVARIANT that every debt increase must pass collateral and gate checks, causing scoped impact: unauthorized over-borrowing via modified market params in ratified offer? Proof idea: fuzz collateralParams LLTV and assert that modified params create a distinct market ID.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Gate or ratifier bypass allowing restricted markets, restricted liquidation, or restricted credit/debt increase to be accessed by unauthorized users'] [Function: setIsRootRatified] Can an attacker ratify a root for an offer with offer.market.maturity = block.timestamp + 1 (near-maturity) to exploit post-maturity liquidation ramp, under PRECONDITIONS where attacker constructs offer with very short maturity and market has liquidatorGate, triggering CALL_SEQUENCE: attacker calls setIsRootRatified(maker, root, true) for near-maturity offer -> attacker takes offer creating debt -> maturity passes -> post-maturity liquidation incentive ramps from 1 to LIFmax over 15 minutes -> attacker liquidates at favorable LIF, violating INVARIANT that liquidator gate must restrict who may liquidate, causing scoped impact: unauthorized liquidation profit via near-maturity offer in gated market? Proof idea: warp to maturity+1 and assert liquidatorGate check applies regardless of ratifier state.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Gate or ratifier bypass allowing restricted markets, restricted liquidation, or restricted credit/debt increase to be accessed by unauthorized users'] [Function: setIsRootRatified] Can an attacker ratify a root for an offer with offer.market.rcfThreshold set to 0 to disable recovery close factor, under PRECONDITIONS where attacker constructs offer with rcfThreshold = 0 and market has liquidatorGate, triggering CALL_SEQUENCE: attacker calls setIsRootRatified(maker, root, true) for offer with rcfThreshold = 0 -> attacker takes offer creating debt -> attacker liquidates full position without close factor restriction, violating INVARIANT that repay amount capped by recovery close factor, causing scoped impact: unauthorized full liquidation bypassing close factor in gated market? Proof idea: unit test asserting rcfThreshold = 0 in offer creates distinct market ID with different liquidation behavior.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Gate or ratifier bypass allowing restricted markets, restricted liquidation, or restricted credit/debt increase to be accessed by unauthorized users'] [Function: setIsRootRatified] Can an attacker ratify a root for multiple offers sharing the same consumption group to exhaust maker's group budget across markets, under PRECONDITIONS where maker has authorized attacker and attacker constructs offers in different markets with same group, triggering CALL_SEQUENCE: attacker calls setIsRootRatified(maker, root, true) for offers in markets A and B with same group -> attacker takes offers in both markets -> consumed[maker][group] accumulates across markets -> maker's intended budget is exceeded, violating INVARIANT that consumption group budget cannot be exceeded across offers, causing scoped impact: unauthorized over-consumption of maker's cross-market group budget? Proof idea: state test asserting consumed[maker][group] is shared across markets and can be exhausted by attacker-ratified offers.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Gate or ratifier bypass allowing restricted markets, restricted liquidation, or restricted credit/debt increase to be accessed by unauthorized users'] [Function: setIsRootRatified] Can an attacker ratify a root for an offer and then use midnight.setConsumed to reset consumed state to enable replay, under PRECONDITIONS where attacker is authorized by maker and calls setConsumed to reset consumed[maker][group] to 0, triggering CALL_SEQUENCE: attacker calls setIsRootRatified(maker, root, true) -> attacker takes offer (consumed increases) -> attacker calls midnight.setConsumed(group, 0, maker) -> attacker takes offer again (consumed resets), violating INVARIANT that cancelled or already-consumed offers cannot be filled, causing scoped impact: offer replay via consumed state reset in restricted market? Proof idea: unit test asserting setConsumed can only increase consumed (AlreadyConsumed check) and cannot enable replay.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Gate or ratifier bypass allowing restricted markets, restricted liquidation, or restricted credit/debt increase to be accessed by unauthorized users'] [Function: setIsRootRatified] Can an attacker ratify a root for an offer and then use midnight.setIsAuthorized to grant themselves additional permissions mid-take via callback, under PRECONDITIONS where attacker is authorized by maker and constructs offer with callback that calls midnight.setIsAuthorized, triggering CALL_SEQUENCE: attacker calls setIsRootRatified(maker, root, true) -> attacker calls midnight.take(offer, ...) -> callback calls midnight.setIsAuthorized(attacker2, true, maker) -> attacker2 gains full control over maker's state, violating INVARIANT that authorization must only allow intended account delegation, causing scoped impact: unauthorized authorization escalation via callback in ratified offer? Proof idea: unit test with callback that calls setIsAuthorized and assert new authorization cannot be used within same take.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Gate or ratifier bypass allowing restricted markets, restricted liquidation, or restricted credit/debt increase to be accessed by unauthorized users'] [Function: setIsRootRatified] Can an attacker ratify a root for an offer with a Merkle tree of height 20 (maximum) and exploit the offerTreeTypeHash to create a type confusion, under PRECONDITIONS where proof.length = 20 and attacker constructs a proof that passes isLeaf for a different offer, triggering CALL_SEQUENCE: attacker calls setIsRootRatified(maker, root, true) for height-20 tree -> attacker constructs proof of length 20 for a different offer -> isLeaf traverses 20 levels -> attacker finds collision in keccak256(left || right) chain, violating INVARIANT that proof verification must reject all offers not in the committed tree, causing scoped impact: unauthorized offer fill via Merkle proof collision in maximum-height tree? Proof idea: fuzz proof elements at height 20 and assert no collision is findable within gas limits.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Gate or ratifier bypass allowing restricted markets, restricted liquidation, or restricted credit/debt increase to be accessed by unauthorized users'] [Function: setIsRootRatified] Can an attacker ratify a root for an offer with duplicate sibling nodes in the proof to create a second-preimage attack, under PRECONDITIONS where proof contains repeated bytes32 values and leafIndex is crafted to traverse the same node twice, triggering CALL_SEQUENCE: attacker calls setIsRootRatified(maker, root, true) for root = hashNode(X, X) -> attacker supplies proof = [X] with leafIndex = 0 for offer with hash X -> isLeaf: currentHash = X, i=0: (0>>0)&1=0 -> hashNode(X, X) = root -> returns true, violating INVARIANT that each offer has a unique position in the tree, causing scoped impact: unauthorized offer fill via second-preimage attack with duplicate siblings? Proof idea: unit test with proof = [X] and leafIndex = 0 for offer hash X in root = hashNode(X, X).

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Gate or ratifier bypass allowing restricted markets, restricted liquidation, or restricted credit/debt increase to be accessed by unauthorized users'] [Function: setIsRootRatified] Can an attacker ratify a root for an offer and then un-ratify it after partial fill to prevent further fills, under PRECONDITIONS where attacker is authorized by maker and offer has been partially filled, triggering CALL_SEQUENCE: attacker calls setIsRootRatified(maker, root, true) -> taker partially fills offer -> attacker calls setIsRootRatified(maker, root, false) -> subsequent fills revert with NotRatified -> offer is effectively cancelled mid-fill, violating INVARIANT that partial fills must update consumed state before any exploitable reentry point, causing scoped impact: DoS of partial fills via mid-fill ratification revocation? Proof idea: state test asserting that un-ratifying a root after partial fill blocks subsequent fills.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Gate or ratifier bypass allowing restricted markets, restricted liquidation, or restricted credit/debt increase to be accessed by unauthorized users'] [Function: setIsRootRatified] Can an attacker ratify a root for an offer with offer.market.enterGate = address(attackerGate) where attackerGate always returns true, bypassing the intended gate restriction, under PRECONDITIONS where the intended market has a restrictive enterGate and attacker creates a shadow market with permissive gate, triggering CALL_SEQUENCE: attacker deploys attackerGate that always returns true -> attacker calls setIsRootRatified(maker, root, true) for offer with market.enterGate = attackerGate -> attacker calls midnight.take(offer, ...) -> touchMarket creates shadow market with permissive gate -> gate check passes for any user, violating INVARIANT that gate checks must apply to all entry paths, causing scoped impact: unauthorized credit/debt increase via shadow market with attacker-controlled gate? Proof idea: unit test asserting that offer.market.enterGate field is included in market ID hash.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Gate or ratifier bypass allowing restricted markets, restricted liquidation, or restricted credit/debt increase to be accessed by unauthorized users'] [Function: setIsRootRatified] Can an attacker ratify a root for an offer with offer.market.enterGate = address(setterRatifier) to create a circular dependency where the gate checks SetterRatifier state, under PRECONDITIONS where SetterRatifier is used as both ratifier and enterGate, triggering CALL_SEQUENCE: attacker calls setIsRootRatified(maker, root, true) for offer with enterGate = setterRatifier -> take() calls setterRatifier.canIncreaseCredit(buyer) -> SetterRatifier does not implement IEnterGate -> call reverts or returns unexpected value, violating INVARIANT that gate checks must apply consistently, causing scoped impact: DoS or gate bypass via circular ratifier-gate dependency? Proof idea: unit test with SetterRatifier as enterGate and assert behavior is well-defined.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Gate or ratifier bypass allowing restricted markets, restricted liquidation, or restricted credit/debt increase to be accessed by unauthorized users'] [Function: setIsRootRatified] Can an attacker ratify a root for an offer and exploit the fact that isRootRatified has no expiry to fill the offer long after the maker's intent has changed, under PRECONDITIONS where maker ratified a root years ago and market conditions have changed, triggering CALL_SEQUENCE: maker calls setIsRootRatified(maker, root, true) at time T -> maker forgets to un-ratify -> attacker calls midnight.take(offer, ratifierData, units, ...) at time T + years -> offer is still fillable if not expired, violating INVARIANT that ratifier approval must reflect current maker intent, causing scoped impact: unauthorized stale offer fill in restricted market? Proof idea: warp to far future and assert that ratified roots without expiry remain valid indefinitely.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Gate or ratifier bypass allowing restricted markets, restricted liquidation, or restricted credit/debt increase to be accessed by unauthorized users'] [Function: setIsRootRatified] Can an attacker ratify a root for an offer with offer.maker = address(0) to bypass the maker authorization check, under PRECONDITIONS where isRootRatified[address(0)][root] can be set by anyone who is authorized by address(0), triggering CALL_SEQUENCE: attacker checks if midnight.isAuthorized(address(0), attacker) is true -> if so, attacker calls setIsRootRatified(address(0), root, true) -> attacker calls midnight.take(offer with maker=address(0), ...) -> isRatified checks isRootRatified[address(0)][root], violating INVARIANT that maker must be a legitimate participant, causing scoped impact: unauthorized offer fill with zero-address maker? Proof idea: unit test asserting that offer.maker = address(0) is rejected by take() or isRatified.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Gate or ratifier bypass allowing restricted markets, restricted liquidation, or restricted credit/debt increase to be accessed by unauthorized users'] [Function: setIsRootRatified] Can an attacker ratify a root for an offer and use the EcrecoverAuthorizer to obtain authorization for setIsRootRatified via a signed authorization message, under PRECONDITIONS where maker has authorized EcrecoverAuthorizer and attacker obtains a signed authorization granting attacker access to maker's state, triggering CALL_SEQUENCE: attacker obtains signed Authorization{authorizer=maker, authorized=attacker, isAuthorized=true} -> attacker calls ecrecoverAuthorizer.setIsAuthorized(auth, sig) -> attacker is now authorized by maker -> attacker calls setterRatifier.setIsRootRatified(maker, maliciousRoot, true), violating INVARIANT that authorization must only allow intended account delegation, causing scoped impact: unauthorized ratification via signature-based authorization escalation? Proof idea: unit test asserting that EcrecoverAuthorizer-granted authorization enables setIsRootRatified on behalf of maker.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Gate or ratifier bypass allowing restricted markets, restricted liquidation, or restricted credit/debt increase to be accessed by unauthorized users'] [Function: setIsRootRatified] Can an attacker ratify a root for an offer in a market with enterGate and then use a taker callback to call setIsRootRatified to un-ratify the root, causing the gate check to fail for subsequent takes in the same block, under PRECONDITIONS where taker callback calls setIsRootRatified(maker, root, false), triggering CALL_SEQUENCE: attacker calls setIsRootRatified(maker, root, true) -> attacker calls midnight.take(offer, ...) with takerCallback -> takerCallback calls setterRatifier.setIsRootRatified(maker, root, false) -> subsequent takes of same offer revert with NotRatified, violating INVARIANT that partial fills must update consumed state before any exploitable reentry point, causing scoped impact: DoS of subsequent fills via taker callback un-ratification? Proof idea: unit test with taker callback that un-ratifies root and assert subsequent fills are blocked.
